text.title: ai-spatial-computing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-spatial-computing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Proposing head-locked UI panels for persistent content display.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Head-locked UI follows every head movement and induces nausea. Agents tend to propose head-locked layouts because they are simpler to specify — always flag and reject.

failure-mode.description: Deploying AI scene understanding in environments with poor lighting or clutter without a default fallback layout.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scene classification accuracy degrades significantly under non-ideal conditions; without a fallback the UI becomes unpredictable.

failure-mode.description: Relying on gesture prediction as the primary input for users with non-standard postures or physical disabilities.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gesture models are trained on normative postures; significant degradation with disability-related movement patterns makes gesture-only interaction exclusionary.

failure-mode.description: Deploying voice integration as the only input modality in public or noisy environments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Voice recognition fails in noisy spaces; a fallback interaction model (gaze + dwell, hand gesture) is mandatory.

failure-mode.description: Personalization models without sufficient usage history.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Personalization models require sufficient usage history; cold-start UX is always degraded. Plan for graceful degradation in early-adoption phases.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring battery and thermal constraints on standalone HMDs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Battery and thermal constraints on standalone HMDs limit AI inference frequency. Design with inference budgets in mind.

failure-mode.description: Agents cannot simulate XR hardware — all spatial ergonomic decisions require human sign-off before build.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents lack access to XR simulators; spatial design validation requires actual hardware or high-fidelity prototyping tools.

failure-mode.description: Scene classification outputs from AI are probabilistic; agent specs must include explicit confidence thresholds and fallback rules.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents may omit confidence thresholds, leading to non-deterministic behavior.

failure-mode.description: LLM hallucination risk is high for platform-specific constraints (e.g., visionOS eye-tracking limits) — always cite official platform docs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Training data lag means agents may cite outdated API versions or incorrect platform limits.

failure-mode.description: Personalization model designs produced by agents need human review for privacy compliance (GDPR/CCPA) before implementation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents may not account for privacy regulations when proposing user data collection and retention patterns.

failure-mode.description: Agents tend to propose head-locked UI (easy to specify) — flag and reject; world-anchored is always preferred.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Head-locked UI is simpler to define algorithmically; agents default to it without understanding nausea risk.
